title: Vandalism

jokes.text: ERIN: Yeah, Darryl's here. So's Santa Claus. It's just a regular Thursday.
jokes.setup: Someone asks if Darryl is around and another person says he's here somewhere
jokes.punchline: Erin equates Darryl's presence with Santa Claus being there on a regular Thursday
jokes.explanation: Strong character-specific humor showing Erin's naive literalness and odd logic. Well-structured misdirection.

jokes.text: Neither guy is here! And it's Friday! Welcome to me and Darryl's world of lies!
jokes.setup: Erin's previous claim that both Darryl and Santa are there
jokes.punchline: She immediately contradicts herself and enthusiastically embraces the deception
jokes.explanation: Perfect Erin character moment - her excitement about lying is both endearing and absurd. Strong escalation.

jokes.text: So now he has to sneak out of here like the sneakiest little sneaky-sneak you ever saw.
jokes.setup: Explaining Darryl's need to be secretive about his Philly job
jokes.punchline: Childish alliteration and repetition of 'sneaky'
jokes.explanation: Classic Erin childish speech pattern. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Darryl, meet Bear-yl.
jokes.setup: Erin introducing something to Darryl
jokes.punchline: Rhyming 'Darryl' with 'Bear-yl' for a stuffed animal
jokes.explanation: Simple rhyming pun. Cute but predictable and not particularly funny.

jokes.text: How much did you pay for that? Nothing. Won him at the carnival. Spent a ton on tickets, though.
jokes.setup: Question about cost of carnival prize
jokes.punchline: Claims it was free but spent a fortune on tickets to win it
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about carnival economics. Well-structured reveal.

jokes.text: But Erin was so excited about being sneaky-sneaks, I went along with it.
jokes.setup: Darryl explaining why he's pretending to sneak around
jokes.punchline: He's indulging Erin's childish excitement about deception
jokes.explanation: Shows Darryl's patience with Erin's quirks. Sweet character moment with mild humor.

jokes.text: I'm usually very self-critical. I hate what I paint, but I don't know, this time I feel like it's, um... It's really coming together. Oh, my God! (EXCLAIMS) You've got to be kidding me! What is... Are those... Are those butts?
jokes.setup: Pam feeling confident about her mural for once
jokes.punchline: Immediate discovery that someone vandalized it with butts
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and payoff. The timing of her rare confidence being immediately crushed is well-crafted.

jokes.text: Oh, cute! So there'll be a bunch of kids? No, no children. Our house is not kid-friendly. Most of our furniture is sharp. Also, ew.
jokes.setup: Someone assumes a child's birthday party will have children
jokes.punchline: Angela's horrified rejection of children at her own son's party
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Angela's character - judgmental, pretentious, and oddly hostile to children at her own child's party.

jokes.text: Angela, I am prepared to donate a whopping $8 to Lipton For America to have an invitation...
jokes.setup: Kevin offering to donate for an invitation
jokes.punchline: The pathetically small amount of $8 presented as 'whopping'
jokes.explanation: Good Kevin characterization - his complete lack of awareness about money and social situations.

jokes.text: You know, actually, none of you could even really make the cut for this thing. Which I am so sad about!
jokes.setup: Angela rejecting Kevin's offer
jokes.punchline: Fake sadness about excluding everyone while clearly being pleased
jokes.explanation: Classic Angela passive-aggression. The transparent insincerity is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: But as a politician in this town, you still need the conventional blonde wife on your lawn signs.
jokes.setup: Oscar explaining his relationship with the Senator
jokes.punchline: Blunt acknowledgment of political image management over genuine relationships
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire with a cynical edge. Oscar's matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Just a couple of grown, sexy-ass roommates.
jokes.setup: Jim describing his living situation with Darryl
jokes.punchline: The awkward 'sexy-ass roommates' description
jokes.explanation: Jim trying too hard to sound cool comes across as awkward and forced, which is the point.

jokes.text: It's just, he uses old T-shirts as washrags. He doesn't wash his dishes. Apparently they need to 'soak.'
jokes.setup: Darryl talking about living with Jim
jokes.punchline: Jim's gross bachelor habits, especially the dishes that perpetually need to 'soak'
jokes.explanation: Relatable roommate humor. The air quotes around 'soak' sell the exasperation perfectly.

jokes.text: Yeah, I don't know everybody's name down here, but whoever did this, will you please raise your hand?
jokes.setup: Pam trying to address the warehouse about vandalism
jokes.punchline: Politely asking vandals to self-identify while admitting she doesn't know anyone's name
jokes.explanation: Shows Pam's naive approach to confrontation and disconnect from warehouse workers.

jokes.text: I will stay up here all day if I have to. Is that what you want?
jokes.setup: Pam trying to sound threatening to the warehouse
jokes.punchline: Weak threat that sounds more like a parent disciplining toddlers
jokes.explanation: Pam's ineffective attempt at intimidation. The childish phrasing undermines any authority.

jokes.text: Yeah, I will also come down if I want to. It's my choice.
jokes.setup: Pam's previous threat to stay upstairs
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermining her own threat by asserting she can also leave
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pam moment showing her inability to maintain any kind of intimidating presence.

jokes.text: I'm not like Angela, who calls a lawyer every time someone watches a YouTube video of animals doing it.
jokes.setup: Pam saying she doesn't demand justice often
jokes.punchline: Specific example of Angela's litigiousness over animal mating videos
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific character detail about Angela's prudishness taken to legal extremes.

jokes.text: Already on it. I ordered them by their Google Trend rankings, so we know who to hit first. Who is this guy? (LAUGHS) We are killing it.
jokes.setup: Work conversation about targeting clients
jokes.punchline: Jim and Darryl's over-the-top enthusiasm about mundane work tasks
jokes.explanation: Shows Jim trying too hard to be the cool work buddy. The fake energy is mildly amusing.

jokes.text: You're telling me David Wallace asked you to call a super-secret, classified conference-room meeting? Yeah. Let's go, everyone! Super-secret, classified conference-room meeting! Now!
jokes.setup: Someone questioning the legitimacy of Clark's meeting call
jokes.punchline: Clark doubling down and loudly announcing the 'super-secret' meeting to everyone
jokes.explanation: The contradiction between 'secret' and shouting it to everyone is a solid bit of irony.

jokes.text: They used worse paint than your paint? I don't think so. But they put paint where I didn't want paint. So... I thought you wanted paint on the whole thing. Different colored paint. I wanted different colored paint in the spots where they put their paint.
jokes.setup: Kevin trying to understand the vandalism problem
jokes.punchline: Increasingly circular and confusing explanation about paint placement
jokes.explanation: Kevin's literal-mindedness creates absurd confusion. Gets a bit wordy but the logic is funny.

jokes.text: It's 6-7-8-2, not 8-3! Uh, 6-7-8-3 is also a good time. Less mileage.
jokes.setup: Creed complaining about his phone number being written on bathroom wall
jokes.punchline: Correcting the number and casually rating both numbers for sexual encounters
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed joke - unexpected, darkly funny, and completely in character. The 'less mileage' detail is brilliant.

jokes.text: I was politely saying nothing can be done. I thought I was clear.
jokes.setup: Pam asking what can be done about the vandalism
jokes.punchline: Oscar's dry clarification that he was already dismissing the situation
jokes.explanation: Great Oscar deadpan. The polite dismissal delivered with perfect condescension.

jokes.text: If there's anything I hate worse than art, it's crime.
jokes.setup: Dwight agreeing to help Pam
jokes.punchline: His backwards priorities - hating art but hating crime slightly more
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Dwight logic delivered with perfect economy. The hierarchy of hatred is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: I believe in you, I believe in your art, and I am bored.
jokes.setup: Nellie explaining why she'll help
jokes.punchline: Deflating the noble sentiments with honest admission of boredom
jokes.explanation: Good structure with the false buildup to honest motivation. Classic Nellie bluntness.

jokes.text: I ended up with Dwight and Nellie. But they both have a mob mentality. And I'm pretty sure Dwight has a pitchfork in his car.
jokes.setup: Pam wanting a righteous mob but getting two people
jokes.punchline: The pitchfork detail suggesting they might be more dangerous than expected
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific detail about Dwight. The pitchfork is believable and funny.

jokes.text: You need my pitchfork? (SIGHS)
jokes.setup: Pam's speculation about Dwight having a pitchfork
jokes.punchline: Dwight immediately offering it, confirming her suspicion
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate callback with Pam's sigh showing she's unsurprised. Great timing.

jokes.text: If I get the de-icing gig, it's going to be on merit, not because I played politics.
jokes.setup: Angela offering Dwight a networking opportunity
jokes.punchline: Dwight's principled stance while literally talking about 'beet-salt' for de-icing
jokes.explanation: Dwight's misguided principles about his ridiculous beet business ventures are perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I am handing out pieces of paper on which you will draw one human butt. What I do with said drawings is no one's business but my own. Incorrect. It is my fetish. Oh, also, sign them. My fetish is signed drawings of butts.
jokes.setup: Dwight's plan to identify the vandal by comparing butt drawings
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting himself and claiming it's his fetish
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dwight moment showing his inability to maintain a cover story. The escalation is perfectly awkward.

jokes.text: All right, then, no bottoms. Uh... We should ask you to do big rounded W's. Yes, or nippleless breasts, perhaps.
jokes.setup: Warehouse workers refusing to draw butts
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous alternatives that all look like butts
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist escalation. The 'nippleless breasts' is a particularly weird but funny image.

jokes.text: Or melons, like cantaloupes, with, like, the halves are cut off and then just the bottom parts...
jokes.setup: Continuing the butt-drawing alternatives
jokes.punchline: Nellie's rambling description getting more awkward and specific
jokes.explanation: Continuation gets a bit wordy and less funny. The cringe factor works but structure suffers.

jokes.text: Your mother is dying! See? I feel bad about that. Good. That's all right, that's all right. So she's going to pull through again?
jokes.setup: Dwight's plan to use fake family emergency to get Nate alone
jokes.punchline: Nate's casual response suggesting this happens regularly
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about family emergencies as manipulation tactic. Nate's casual acceptance is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Gum's gotten mintier lately, have you noticed? Like, some of it is just too minty. It's like they're literally trying to hurt your mouth.
jokes.setup: Interrogating Nate about vandalism
jokes.punchline: Nate completely derailing with random observation about gum
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nate non-sequitur. The specific complaint about mint intensity is hilariously random and relatable.

jokes.text: There's no gum. There never was any gum! That's really rude.
jokes.setup: Dwight's interrogation tactic using promised gum
jokes.punchline: Nate's genuine hurt about the gum deception
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the gum setup. Nate's priority confusion (caring more about gum than the lies) is funny.

jokes.text: Opening with puff pastries? That's a bold play. They're saying, 'it's only going to get better from here.' Good luck.
jokes.setup: Oscar at Angela's upscale party
jokes.punchline: Snooty analysis of appetizer strategy with implied criticism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Oscar pretentiousness. The food criticism disguised as party analysis is expertly condescending.

jokes.text: How can anyone that weighs less than a guinea hen be so terrifying?
jokes.setup: Oscar looking at something at the party
jokes.punchline: Comparing someone's tiny size to a guinea hen while noting their intimidating presence
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and unusual comparison. The guinea hen reference is unexpected and perfectly Oscar-esque.

jokes.text: Oh, man, that's a bummer. I'm sorry about that. No big deal, no big deal. No, if it's a big deal, it's a big deal. No,no,no. No big deal.
jokes.setup: Jim accidentally using Darryl's thermos
jokes.punchline: Awkward over-apologizing cycle where both try too hard to be considerate
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing roommate tension. The repetitive structure captures the awkwardness well.

jokes.text: I don't know. Are you going to wash it, or you gonna let it soak?
jokes.setup: Jim offering to wash the thermos
jokes.punchline: Darryl's passive-aggressive reference to Jim's dish-soaking habit
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback with perfect passive-aggressive delivery. The tension is palpable and relatable.

jokes.text: I'm sorry I didn't like your crappy doodles. I drew a butt. Big deal. Butts are funny. Well, I didn't think that butt was funny. Well, maybe if you got the stick out of yours...
jokes.setup: Pam trying to get Frank to apologize
jokes.punchline: Frank's non-apology escalating to crude insult
jokes.explanation: Solid escalation showing Frank's hostility. The stick/butt wordplay is crude but effective.

jokes.text: The first 'sorry' sounded sincere. There were two or three sorrys in there. So it's a lot.
jokes.setup: Frank's hostile non-apology
jokes.punchline: Nellie's positive spin on counting the word 'sorry' regardless of context
jokes.explanation: Great Nellie logic - focusing on quantity over quality of apologies. The optimism is absurdly misplaced.

jokes.text: Normally, I find Pam to be a comforting if unarousing presence around the office, like a well-watered fern. But today, she has tapped into this vengeful, violent side, and I'm like, 'Wow, Pam has kind of a good butt!'
jokes.setup: Dwight's confessional about Pam's usual demeanor
jokes.punchline: Dwight being turned on by Pam's vengeful side, ending with inappropriate butt comment
jokes.explanation: Peak Dwight creepiness with the plant comparison and inappropriate attraction to violence. Perfectly uncomfortable.

jokes.text: Go all Hammurabi on this clown.
jokes.setup: Discussion about revenge tactics
jokes.punchline: Nellie's pretentious historical reference mixed with casual insult
jokes.explanation: Good Nellie mix of intellectual pretension and crude language. The historical reference is unexpected.

jokes.text: And if you ask me, it's time we bid bigotry hasta luego.
jokes.setup: Robert giving speech about embracing Hispanic people
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt to use Spanish while being transparently political
jokes.explanation: Shows Robert's pandering politician nature. The forced Spanish is appropriately cringey.

jokes.text: His gardener, Rohelio, or he could have invited... Rohelio, but he chose me. Rohelio's Malaysian. That son of a bitch is Malaysian.
jokes.setup: Oscar listing other Hispanic people Robert could have invited
jokes.punchline: Realizing his example isn't even Hispanic and getting indignant
jokes.explanation: Great Oscar moment showing his assumptions being wrong. The escalating indignation is perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: I think you might be going a little crazy with this labeling thing, man. I mean, you put your name on a five-pound bag of flour. Are you honestly saying that if I needed flour, I couldn't use that? What do you need flour for, Jim? That's not the point. What, you making bread? No, I'm not making bread. What bread you making? Pumpernickel?
jokes.setup: Jim criticizing Darryl's food labeling
jokes.punchline: Darryl's increasingly specific questions about Jim's hypothetical bread-making
jokes.explanation: Good roommate tension escalation. Darryl's specific bread questions derail Jim's argument effectively.

jokes.text: What happened to my Tavis Smileys? Oh, crap, were those yours?
jokes.setup: The ongoing roommate food conflicts
jokes.punchline: Jim's sheepish admission after eating Darryl's food
jokes.explanation: Solid escalation of roommate tension. The 'Tavis Smileys' reference adds character specificity.

jokes.text: But my only crime was loving the local sports teams and trying to be one of the guys! Silence! You will now be working in the warehouse with the untouchables.
jokes.setup: Clark's fake punishment scenario
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic exile with caste system reference
jokes.explanation: Good theatrical punishment scene. The 'untouchables' reference adds historical weight to the joke.

jokes.text: And why would I be asking that? So we know which truck to... Hey, while I got you on the phone, your husband is like a sloppy homeless hobo. Can you fix that?
jokes.setup: Darryl helping with the truck revenge plan
jokes.punchline: Abruptly switching to complaining about Jim to Pam
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Darryl's frustration spilling over. The timing and bluntness work well.

jokes.text: He put you right in front of me? Yes, he did. He put you right in front of me? Yes! Now, let's just wheel Margaret right in front here. Ow!
jokes.setup: Photo positioning conflicts at the party
jokes.punchline: Escalating blocking with wheelchair placement
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy escalation. The wheelchair addition adds awkwardness but isn't exceptional.

jokes.text: And he's leaving a trail of poops? Yeah. And he has saggy boobs. Yeah, I saw that. That's great!
jokes.setup: Discussing Dwight's drawing on Frank's truck
jokes.punchline: Pam's enthusiastic approval of the crude revenge art
jokes.explanation: Shows Pam's descent into petty revenge. The crude drawings are appropriately juvenile.

jokes.text: I like hanging out with a vengeful bitch. I know. You miss Angela, don't you? (GROANS) Don't sympathize. You're ruining the mood. Back to work! Draw his penis!
jokes.setup: Dwight enjoying Pam's vengeful side
jokes.punchline: Pam connecting it to missing Angela and escalating the artwork
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Pam's transformation and Dwight's weird preferences. The Angela connection is insightful.

jokes.text: Kevin, great to see you. Yeah. Thank you for the food. Oh, and also, you suck. I beg your pardon? You're, like, a terrible person. These guys care about you, and you're just using them.
jokes.setup: Kevin being polite at the party
jokes.punchline: Abrupt shift to brutal honesty about Robert being terrible
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Kevin moment. The politeness followed by brutal truth-telling is perfectly executed and surprising.

jokes.text: Oh! Come on! Oh! Oh! Damn! 'You Win!'
jokes.setup: Jim playing some kind of game
jokes.punchline: Apparent victory followed by pain and ironic 'You Win!' message
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment. The irony is mild and the structure is functional but not exceptional.

tmdb_overview: Pam's mural gets defaced; Darryl adjusts to Jim's habits; Angela hosts her son's party.
